title: Low Volume Methodology for Nextera DNA Flex Library Prep Kit
author: Eric Adams
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.be6rjhd6
materials: Nextera DNA Flex Library Prep Kit

steps.title: Equilibrate the following reagents at Room...
steps.instruction: Equilibrate the following reagents at Room temperature for 00:30:00 Bead-Linked Transposomes (BLT) Tagmentation Buffer (TB1) Tagment Stop Buffer (TSB) Tagment Wash Buffer (TWB) Sample Purification Beads (SPB)

steps.title: Thaw the Resuspension Buffer (RSB) and...
steps.instruction: Thaw the Resuspension Buffer (RSB) and bring to Room temperature then vortex to mix

steps.title: Prepare a proteinase K solution at...
steps.instruction: Prepare a proteinase K solution at 300 μg/mL For a 1mL solution: add 15μL of 20mg/mL proteinase K to 985μL of nuclease-free water

steps.title: Thaw KAPA HiFi HotStart ReadyMix (2X) On ice
steps.instruction: Thaw KAPA HiFi HotStart ReadyMix (2X) On ice

steps.title: Thaw the i5 and i7 indexes...
steps.instruction: Thaw the i5 and i7 indexes at Room temperature Index tubes: vortex and spin down briefly Index plates: spin down at 1500 rpm, °C, to ensure no droplets remain on the plate cover Index stocks should be diluted to 10 micromolar (µM) working stocks

steps.title: Prepare a Tagmentation Master Mix (TMM)...
steps.instruction: Prepare a Tagmentation Master Mix (TMM) TMM contains 1μL BLT and 1μL TB1 per reaction Vortex BLT vigorously before use and ensure the beads are evenly resuspended

steps.title: Prepare PCR Master Mix (PCR MM)...
steps.instruction: Prepare PCR Master Mix (PCR MM) On ice PMM contains 2.75μL nuclease-free water, 0.5μL KAPA-PCR-F primer, 0.5μL KAPA-PCR-R primer, and 6.25μL KAPA HiFi HotStart ReadyMix (2X) per reaction See Guidelines section for KAPA primer design KAPA primer stocks should be diluted to 10 micromolar (µM)

steps.title: Prepare a diluted SPB Master Mix...
steps.instruction: Prepare a diluted SPB Master Mix (SPB MM) 54 µL, SPB + 48 µL, nuclease-free water

steps.title: Make a 110X mix
steps.instruction: Make a 110X mix: 110 µL, TB1 + 110 µL, BLT Vortex to mix

steps.title: Make a 110X mix
steps.instruction: Make a 110X mix: 302.5 µL, nuclease-free water + 55 µL, KAPA-PCR-F + 55 µL, KAPA-PCR-R + 687.5 µL, KAPA HiFi HotStart ReadyMix (2X) Vortex to mix and briefly spin down

steps.title: Transfer at least 100 ng, genomic...
steps.instruction: Transfer at least 100 ng, genomic DNA (up to 500ng) into each well of a 96-well PCR plate in 1-5μL

steps.title: Adjust the volume of each well...
steps.instruction: Adjust the volume of each well to 5 µL, total volume using nuclease-free water

steps.title: Load 27 µL, TMM into each...
steps.instruction: Load 27 µL, TMM into each tube of an 8-strip of PCR tubes Vortex TMM thoroughly before use and ensure the beads remain evenly suspended while being aliquoted into the 96-well plate

steps.title: Add 2 µL, TMM to each...
steps.instruction: Add 2 µL, TMM to each well using an 8-channel pipette Thoroughly mix by gentle pipetting If beads are adhered to the top or side of the 96-well plate, briefly centrifuge and fully resuspend the bead pellet by pipetting until thoroughly mixed

steps.title: Seal the plate with a PCR...
steps.instruction: Seal the plate with a PCR plate cover, place in a thermal cycler, and run the TAGflex program It is especially important to be able to remove the plate seal as easily as possible to prevent cross contamination Proceed immediately to the next steps upon completion of the TAGflex cycle

steps.title: Load 15 µL, TSB per tube...
steps.instruction: Load 15 µL, TSB per tube in an 8-strip of PCR tubes

steps.title: Gently remove the plate seal and...
steps.instruction: Gently remove the plate seal and add 1 µL, TSB into each tagmentation reaction using an 8-channel pipette Mix by gently pipetting the entire volume, ensuring the beads are fully resuspend

steps.title: Seal the plate, place in a...
steps.instruction: Seal the plate, place in a thermal cycler, and run the TAGstop program

steps.title: Remove the seal and place the...
steps.instruction: Remove the seal and place the plate on a 96-well magnetic plate stand for 00:03:00 or until solution is clear

steps.title: Carefully remove the supernatant using an...
steps.instruction: Carefully remove the supernatant using an 8-channel pipette without disturbing the beads

steps.title: Load 6 mL, TWB into a...
steps.instruction: Load 6 mL, TWB into a sterile trough to pipette from

steps.title: Remove the plate from the magnetic...
steps.instruction: Remove the plate from the magnetic stand and add 20 µL, TWB to each well using an 8-channel pipette Mix by gently pipetting (slowly, to minimize foaming) until beads are fully resuspended

steps.title: Place the plate on the magnetic...
steps.instruction: Place the plate on the magnetic stand for 00:03:00 or until solution is clear

steps.title: Carefully remove the supernatant using an...
steps.instruction: Carefully remove the supernatant using an 8-channel pipette without disturbing the beads

steps.title: Repeat steps 20-22 one more time...
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 20-22 one more time for a total of 2 washes

steps.title: Remove the plate from the magnetic...
steps.instruction: Remove the plate from the magnetic stand and add 20 µL, TWB to each well Gently pipette until beads are fully resuspended

steps.title: Seal the plate and place on...
steps.instruction: Seal the plate and place on the magnetic stand to incubate until needed in the next section Keeping the pellet in TWB helps prevent over-drying of the beads

steps.title: Load 135 µL, PCR MM per...
steps.instruction: Load 135 µL, PCR MM per tube into an 8-strip of PCR tubes (store On ice until needed)

steps.title: Carefully remove the TWB supernatant from...
steps.instruction: Carefully remove the TWB supernatant from the samples while on the magnetic stand Any remaining foam on the well walls should not adversely affect the library

steps.title: Remove the plate from the magnetic...
steps.instruction: Remove the plate from the magnetic stand and proceed immediately to the next step to prevent excessive drying of the beads

steps.title: Add 10 µL, PCR MM to...
steps.instruction: Add 10 µL, PCR MM to each sample and pipette the mix to ensure the beads are thoroughly resuspended Pipette with moderate force as the beads can be difficult to fully resuspend

steps.title: Seal the plate and spin down...
steps.instruction: Seal the plate and spin down briefly up to 1000 rpm, °C,

steps.title: Add 2
steps.instruction: Add 2.5 µL of an equimolar mix of each i7 and i5 index combination to their respective wells Individual index tubes: 1.25μL i7 index (N7xx) + 1.25μL i5 index (N5xx) Pre-mixed index plate: Transfer 2.5μL of each index combination using an 8-channel pipette

steps.title: Pipette to mix a minimum of...
steps.instruction: Pipette to mix a minimum of 10 times, then seal the plate and breifly spin up to 1000 rpm, °C,

steps.title: Place the plate in a thermal...
steps.instruction: Place the plate in a thermal cycler and run the flexPCR program

steps.title: Remove the plate from the thermal...
steps.instruction: Remove the plate from the thermal cycler and spin down for 00:01:00 at 280 x g, °C,

steps.title: Load 35 µL of 300 μg/mL,...
steps.instruction: Load 35 µL of 300 μg/mL, proteinase K per tube into an 8-strip of PCR tubes

steps.title: Remove the plate seal and add 2
steps.instruction: Remove the plate seal and add 2.5 µL, proteinase K to each PCR reaction using an 8-channel pipette Mix the wells by pipetting a 10uL volume at least ten times

steps.title: Seal the plate, place in a...
steps.instruction: Seal the plate, place in a thermal cycler, and run the protK program

steps.title: If stopping, keep the plate sealed...
steps.instruction: If stopping, keep the plate sealed and store at 4°C for up to 3 days Otherwise, continue with the next section

steps.title: Remove the seal and place the...
steps.instruction: Remove the seal and place the plate on the magnetic stand for 00:05:00 or until the supernatant is clear

steps.title: Using an 8-channel pipette, transfer 2...
steps.instruction: Using an 8-channel pipette, transfer 2 µL, PCR supernatant from each well into an 8-strip of PCR tubes

steps.title: From the 8-strip of PCR tubes,...
steps.instruction: From the 8-strip of PCR tubes, transfer all samples to a single 1.5mL Eppendorf tube Each tube in the strip should contain ~24μL of pooled sample, yielding ~192μL of pooled libraries

steps.title: Vortex and briefly spin down to...
steps.instruction: Vortex and briefly spin down to mix the pooled libraries evenly

steps.title: Transfer a 45 µL, aliquot of...
steps.instruction: Transfer a 45 µL, aliquot of the pooled libraries to a new 1.5mL Eppendorf tube

steps.title: Vortex and invert SPB MM multiple...
steps.instruction: Vortex and invert SPB MM multiple times to ensure full resuspension

steps.title: Add 85 µL, SPB MM to...
steps.instruction: Add 85 µL, SPB MM to the pooled library aliquot Pipette until thoroughly mixed Complete mixing is critical to proper size distribution of libraries

steps.title: Incubate at Room temperature for 00:05:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at Room temperature for 00:05:00

steps.title: Place the Eppendorf tube on a...
steps.instruction: Place the Eppendorf tube on a magnetic stand for 00:05:00 or until the supernatant is clear

steps.title: Transfer 120 µL, supernatant into a new 1
steps.instruction: Transfer 120 µL, supernatant into a new 1.5mL tube without disturbing the beads

steps.title: Vortex and invert the SPB (undiluted stock) until thoroughly resuspended
steps.instruction: Vortex and invert the SPB (undiluted stock) until thoroughly resuspended

steps.title: Add 14
steps.instruction: Add 14.4 µL, SPB (undiluted stock) to the new 1.5mL tube containing the supernatant

steps.title: Pipette a 120μL volume until thoroughly mixed
steps.instruction: Pipette a 120μL volume until thoroughly mixed

steps.title: Incubate at Room temperature for 00:05:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at Room temperature for 00:05:00

steps.title: Place the Eppendorf tube on a...
steps.instruction: Place the Eppendorf tube on a magnetic stand for 00:05:00 or until clear

steps.title: Remove and discard the supernatant without disturbing the beads
steps.instruction: Remove and discard the supernatant without disturbing the beads

steps.title: With the tube on the magnetic...
steps.instruction: With the tube on the magnetic stand, add 200 µL, 80% ethanol without mixing and incubate for 30s Add enough 80% ethanol so that the beads are entirely submerged

steps.title: Pipette to remove the ethanol without disturbing the beads
steps.instruction: Pipette to remove the ethanol without disturbing the beads

steps.title: Repeat steps 55-56 one more time...
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 55-56 one more time for a total of 2 washes

steps.title: Remove any excess liquid from the...
steps.instruction: Remove any excess liquid from the tube using a pipette without disturbing the beads

steps.title: Air-dry beads on the magnetic stand for 00:05:00
steps.instruction: Air-dry beads on the magnetic stand for 00:05:00

steps.title: Warm 35 µL, RSB at 50...
steps.instruction: Warm 35 µL, RSB at 50 °C to increase final library yield

steps.title: Remove the 1
steps.instruction: Remove the 1.5mL tube from the magnetic stand

steps.title: Add 32 µL, RSB (warm) to...
steps.instruction: Add 32 µL, RSB (warm) to the beads Pipette mix until thoroughly resuspended

steps.title: Incubate on the bench at Room temperature for 00:02:00
steps.instruction: Incubate on the bench at Room temperature for 00:02:00

steps.title: Place the 1
steps.instruction: Place the 1.5mL tube back on the magnetic stand for 00:02:00 or until clear

steps.title: Transfer 30 µL, supernatant into a new 1
steps.instruction: Transfer 30 µL, supernatant into a new 1.5mL Eppendorf tube

steps.title: Let the tube with the eluted...
steps.instruction: Let the tube with the eluted library sit at 50 °C for 00:05:00 to ensure complete dissolution of the library

steps.title: Quantify the library using a Qubit...
steps.instruction: Quantify the library using a Qubit fluorometer Determine fragment size distribution using a Bioanalyzer
